refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR to explicitly state the performance metric (packet loss) and the operational context (agreed network conditions) clarifies the requirement for design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and compare against customer-defined limits.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant modules for operational awareness.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement robust error correction and retransmission protocols in communication stack.
DP2: Use adaptive bitrate and packet prioritization algorithms.
DP3: Integrate real-time network quality monitoring and feedback control.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement robust error correction and retransmission protocols in communication stack.

potential_sources: Interaction between communication stack and teleoperation control logic.
Dependency on network link quality and telemetry data.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically isolates communication quality management, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk of increased latency due to retransmissions impacting teleoperation responsiveness.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating adaptive bitrate (DP2) if retransmission overhead causes unacceptable latency.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation control and communication management.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection used for teleoperation data exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Incoming telemetry data used to monitor system and network status.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad system scope but less focused on communication specifics.
Primarily physical vehicle components, minimal communication logic.
High containment as communication protocols and teleoperation logic co-located.
Focus on safety alerts, less relevant to communication quality.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing communication quality management within the Teleoperation module reduces diffusion and improves maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential overload of Teleoperation module with communication and control responsibilities.
Dependency on Network Link interface reliability.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor module complexity; consider splitting communication management if diffusion risk increases.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP directly addresses the refined FR with clear mapping and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Potential complexity in Teleoperation module due to combined communication and control logic.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as best ownership candidate.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry and network link monitoring is feasible.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of communication quality responsibility to Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Evaluate latency impact of retransmission protocols in next design iteration.
Consider adaptive bitrate strategies if packet loss control impacts responsiveness.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition is sound, risks related to latency and module complexity warrant refinement before proceeding.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant modules for operational awareness.

revise.why: To incorporate adaptive bitrate and latency mitigation strategies alongside error correction.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Teleoperation module can handle added communication management complexity.
open_risks: Latency increase due to retransmission protocols.
Potential module diffusion if communication logic grows.
evidence_needed: Module complexity assessment after DP implementation.
